req.title: Card freeze / unfreeze (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall freeze or unfreeze their card instantly; frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-035 mandates instant card freeze/unfreeze with authorization rejection on frozen cards, boundary behaviour at min/max thresholds, 404 on missing card, and authorization network notification. I derived five tests: (1) EP-positive: freeze an active card and confirm state + network notification; (2) STC-positive: full freeze→unfreeze round-trip confirming state machine; (3) NEG-negative: 404 on non-existent card ID; (4) DT-negative: frozen card rejects an authorization attempt; (5) BVA-boundary: freeze/unfreeze at the exact maximum card-ID boundary value. Each test resets state via the unfreeze endpoint or uses a dedicated fixture to remain idempotent.

tests.name: test_freeze_active_card_returns_200_and_notifies_network
preconditions: Authenticated customer token is available via <auth_token_fixture>
Card <ACTIVE_CARD_ID> exists, belongs to caller, and is currently unfrozen
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
Query authorization network stub for notification event
expected: Authorization network stub received exactly one FREEZE event for the card
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_freeze_active_card_returns_200_and_notifies_network(
    api_client,          # <fixture>
    active_card_id,      # <fixture> yields unfrozen card ID, teardown unfreezes
    auth_network_stub,   # <fixture> captures outbound network calls
):
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{active_card_id}/freeze",
        json={"action": "freeze"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "frozen"

    events = auth_network_stub.get_events(card_id=active_card_id, event_type="FREEZE")
    assert len(events) == 1

tests.name: test_freeze_then_unfreeze_card_state_transition
preconditions: Authenticated customer token is available via <auth_token_fixture>
Card <ACTIVE_CARD_ID> exists, belongs to caller, and is currently unfrozen
expected: State machine transitions: active → frozen → active
tests.executable:
def test_freeze_then_unfreeze_card_state_transition(
    api_client,
    active_card_id,   # <fixture>
):
    freeze_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{active_card_id}/freeze",
        json={"action": "freeze"},
    )
    assert freeze_resp.status_code == 200
    assert freeze_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen"

    unfreeze_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{active_card_id}/freeze",
        json={"action": "unfreeze"},
    )
    assert unfreeze_resp.status_code == 200
    assert unfreeze_resp.json()["status"] == "active"

tests.name: test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404
preconditions: Authenticated customer token is available via <auth_token_fixture>
Card ID <NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID> does not exist in the system
expected: Response body contains error code or message indicating card not found
trace: R-FIN-035: error pathway — 404 when card does not exist
tests.executable:
NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"

def test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404(api_client):
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID}/freeze",
        json={"action": "freeze"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 404
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

tests.name: test_frozen_card_rejects_authorization_attempt
preconditions: Authenticated customer token is available via <auth_token_fixture>
Card <ACTIVE_CARD_ID> exists and is currently frozen via setup step
actions: POST /cards/{ACTIVE_CARD_ID}/freeze to ensure card is frozen
POST /authorizations with payload referencing the frozen card
expected: Authorization endpoint returns HTTP 402 or 403 indicating card is frozen
Response body contains decline reason referencing frozen status
trace: R-FIN-035: frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts
tests.executable:
def test_frozen_card_rejects_authorization_attempt(
    api_client,
    active_card_id,   # <fixture> teardown unfreezes card
):
    freeze_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{active_card_id}/freeze",
        json={"action": "freeze"},
    )
    assert freeze_resp.status_code == 200

    auth_resp = api_client.post(
        "/authorizations",
        json={"card_id": active_card_id, "amount": 100, "currency": "USD"},
    )
    assert auth_resp.status_code in (402, 403)
    body = auth_resp.json()
    decline_reason = body.get("decline_reason", "").lower()
    assert "frozen" in decline_reason or "card_frozen" in body.get("code", "")

tests.name: test_freeze_at_max_card_id_boundary_value
preconditions: Authenticated customer token is available via <auth_token_fixture>
A card provisioned with the maximum allowed numeric card ID exists and is unfrozen
MAX_CARD_ID is defined as the largest valid identifier accepted by the system schema
actions: POST /cards/{MAX_CARD_ID}/freeze with body {"action": "unfreeze"} to restore state
expected: HTTP 200 with status=frozen for the max boundary card ID
Subsequent unfreeze returns HTTP 200 with status=active
No overflow or unexpected error at the boundary value
trace: R-FIN-035: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold must be tested
tests.executable:
MAX_CARD_ID = <MAX_CARD_ID_CONSTANT>  # <helper_fn> returns system max card ID

def test_freeze_at_max_card_id_boundary_value(
    api_client,
    max_boundary_card,   # <fixture> provisions card at MAX_CARD_ID, teardown unfreezes
):
    freeze_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{MAX_CARD_ID}/freeze",
        json={"action": "freeze"},
    )
    assert freeze_resp.status_code == 200
    assert freeze_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen"

    unfreeze_resp = api_client.post(
        f"/cards/{MAX_CARD_ID}/freeze",
        json={"action": "unfreeze"},
    )
    assert unfreeze_resp.status_code == 200
    assert unfreeze_resp.json()["status"] == "active"
